programme.title: Jewels of the Med
programme.desc: Hvar is the longest and sunniest island in Croatia. Home to over 10,000 residents, known for its fields of lavender, rare and remarkable lace making, its secluded bays with beautiful beaches and six Unesco heritage listings. It's an island associated with luxury and beauty, a magnet for the rich and the famous. One of the island's most glamorous hangouts can be found in the capital, owned by Ivan Gospodnetic, and renowned for its seafood. Each day Ivan sends out a fleet of seven fishing boats. In 1868, Hvar became the birthplace of organised tourism in Europe which saw an explosion in popularity in the 1960s thanks to The Hvar Hygienic Society, a group who promoted the island as a health retreat. The Hvar Summer Festival is one of the oldest summer festivals on the Adriatic coast. This year marks the first time Jelena oversees the running of the festival's programme. Narrated by Joanna Lumley. Exec Prods: Dympna Jackson/ Melanie Darlaston; Purple Productions/GroupM Motion Entertainment Limited
Joanna Lumley narrates this exploration of Mediterranean islands, cuisine and culture.

programme.title: Jane McDonald: From Pole to Pole

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben visits northern England for a journey into a different kind of wild living - 'continuous cruising' on a narrowboat - staying onboard with his host Dave, whose move to an off-grid life on the Cheshire canals became a lifeline after personal tragedy. Ben discovers how the inland waterways offered Dave not just solace, but a renewed sense of purpose, introducing him to the rhythms of living afloat and to the extraordinary wildlife the canals support, which has helped shape his new way of life. The pair experience the contrast between a slower pace of life and the physical demands of boat living. As Ben takes on boat maintenance and adapts to the challenges of the waterways, he comes to appreciate how this unique journey through nature has helped restore his host. Exec Prod: Natalie Wilkinson; Dir: Natalie Wilkinson; Rushlight Films (S21 Ep4)

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: It is Christmas time on Ravenseat Farm, and all nine children are home for the holidays. Four-year-old Clemmy has her hands full nursing a premature calf.

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Royal Residences: A Map of Britain
programme.desc: A revealing look inside King Charles's staggering multi-billion-pound property portfolio of spectacular castles, palaces and farmhouses, stretching across the UK and beyond.
A revealing look inside King Charles's staggering multi-billion-pound property portfolio.

programme.title: Secrets of The Royal Traditions
programme.desc: We investigate how the New Year's and Birthday Honours came about from the vicious savagery of medieval warfare to the horrors of World War One. We also reveal how knighthood is steeped in ceremonial ritual and harks back to a time of chivalry that never actually existed. For the royals, one of the more unfortunate traditions they have to endure is the scandal and by examining what created Queen Elizabeth II's 'Annus Horibilis' and the divorce from hell between George IV and his wife, we discover how the royals contribute - and recover - from these regular but inconvenient occurrences. And the tradition of the royal servant is explored - a role that stretches back as long as royalty itself, including the role of the royal jester. Prod/Dir: Simon Newton; Series Prod/Dir: Michael Douglas; Exec Prod: Oliver Wright (S2 Ep3)
Revealing how the monarchy uses traditions to maintain a place in our nation's identity.

programme.title: Kate: A Life in 10 Dresses
programme.desc: This is the story of one of the world's most recognisable women told through ten of her most famous dresses. The Princess of Wales is a style icon, but for her, clothes are about so much more than looking good. They're how she tells us who she is and what she cares about. In this film, through ten important dresses, we mark ten milestones on Kate's path to becoming the princess she is today, from her much-copied sapphire blue engagement dress to the regal lace gown she wore at the state banquet for President Trump in 2025, her wedding dress to the cloaked Coronation gown, and more. With multiple contributors who know Kate well, this film celebrates the fashion choices of the world's most photographed woman, and the stories these dresses tell us about her extraordinary transformation into our future Queen. Exec Prods: Janet Lee/Gabriel Jagger; Dir: Emily Kennedy; whynow

programme.title: Most Shocking Celebrity Moments

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: After reports come in of a cloned car, cops get behind the suspect car and start putting together a plan. When the driver is detained and questioned, cops find that he doesn't have a licence or insurance. And when the car is searched, something else illegal is found that changes everything. While on patrol near Grimsby, cops watch in shock as a motorbike dangerously weaves in and out of traffic. But the rider refuses to see anything wrong with what he's done, leaving the cops questioning if there is another motive for his shocking riding. And on the outskirts of Grimsby, PCs on the lookout for drunk drivers spot a car that arouses suspicion. Series Prod: Johnny Perks; Exec Prods: Zoë Callan/Dympna Jackson; Dirs: Sam Knowles/John Nichols/Phil Hirst/Emma Fraser; Purple Productions (S9 Ep2)
Ride along with the officers risking their lives policing Britain's most dangerous roads.

programme.title: Platoon 24/7: Preparing for War
programme.desc: When news arrives that Donald Trump intends to stop sharing American military intelligence with Ukraine, Keir Starmer says that Britain is ready to deploy a peacekeeping force within weeks. As Armoured Infantry, 13 Platoon could be some of the first deployed. Their schedule now moves into training for Eastern European combat. Their first challenge is urban warfare with an assault on a training village on the Salisbury Plain Training Area. Soon after, they're deployed to a NATO training area in Germany. Here they train in their Warrior armoured fighting vehicles - tank-like machines which can carry Riflemen inside. Each Warrior crew will need to pass a live firing test by destroying moving targets. When the physical nature of the drills causes the platoon to go a man down, Corporal Sherborne must motivate the others to pass a final assessment - an attack against an enemy at night. Exec Prods: Martin Oxley/Jamie Balment; Dir: Will Lorimer; Frank Films and Motion Entertainment Production (S1 Ep2/3)
We follow six gruelling months as a platoon prepares for possible frontline deployment.

programme.desc: A couple have been looking forward to enjoying their golden years together, caravanning with their spaniels Zach and Ollie. But after Ollie had an operation, the brothers had a big falling-out and have been at war ever since. Can Graeme Hall rescue their retirement? Randy Rupert the labradoodle has only one thing on his mind... trying to get his leg over with the lady of the house. Mum-of-two Bobbi has had enough of this shaggy boy's unrequited love, and with a toddler and baby to look after, she's at the end of her tether. Plus, Graeme offers top tips on doggie nail clipping. programme.title: The Good Ship Murder
programme.desc: Two old flames, Alex and Lucia, reunite in the Ibiza nightclub where they first kissed some 20 years previously. Alex is on a lads' holiday with his lairy mate Gary, and Lucia now co-owns the club. But when Lucia's slimy business associate Rico is murdered - pushed off the balcony at the club - Jack and Kate get on the case. Various suspects emerge, including Lucia, and local mafia boss Carmen Rivera, as well as her henchman Paco. Meanwhile, in the wake of learning about Marlowe's illness, Jack remains at odds with Kate, but he can't stay mad with her for long and the pair eventually reach a new understanding. Writer: Kat Rose-Martin; Series Prod: Jonathan Phillips; Prod/Dir: Steve Hughes; Exec Prods: Andy Morgan/Paul Matthew Thompson; Clapperboard Studios (S3 Ep7)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle heads to the island of Tenerife to visit young couple Emma and Edo, whose ambition to rescue a few horses created a very different wild sanctuary. He discovers how this Italian and Brit met and ended up with hundreds of animals on this barren and rocky patch of the Canary Islands. He learns that the couple's dream grew quickly into a haven for more than just their animals and that life on their community farm is also a multi-generational family affair. Ben finds himself knee-deep in the daily realities of 'finca' life and begins to understand what draws volunteers to this place and to its extraordinary hosts. Ben experiences first-hand what life is like for the pair as young parents, where their dreams of running an eco-farm, have turned into running an entire village. Exec Prod: Natalie Wilkinson; Dir: Jo Young; Rushlight Films (S21 Ep5)
